page: Amazon Web Services m5ad.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores based on the x86_64 AMD EPYC 7571 processor, operating at a clock speed of 2.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes a bundled 600 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 5 Gbps, and there are no integrated GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance profile with notable tradeoffs. Single-core performance is a limitation, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 10%. Multi-core performance is slightly stronger but remains modest, with stress-ng multi-core and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression ranking in the bottom 25%, while Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU scores reach average levels. Memory performance is mixed; read-only small block cached bandwidth is average, but large block uncached read and write bandwidths fall into the bottom 25%.

The inclusion of a 600 GB local NVMe SSD provides a resource density advantage for workloads requiring fast local scratch space or temporary database storage. Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations both perform at average levels, making this instance suitable for moderate database tasks, web serving, and general-purpose applications that do not demand high single-threaded CPU performance. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the use of AMD EPYC processors and bundled local NVMe storage offers a balanced resource-to-cost ratio for multi-threaded workloads that can tolerate lower single-core speeds.
description: Amazon Web Services m5ad.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.2 GHz. It is configured with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a bundled 600 GB NVMe SSD local instance store, operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a 5 Gbps baseline network speed. Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct tradeoff: single-core CPU execution and uncached memory bandwidth are weak, ranking in the bottom 10% to 25%, whereas multi-core CPU metrics, Passmark database operations, and Redis SET operations achieve average tier results. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for multi-threaded applications and moderate database workloads that benefit from local NVMe scratch space but do not require high single-threaded compute performance.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5ad.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 600 GB NVMe SSD, offering average multi-core and database performance with weaker single-core speeds.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5ad.4xlarge features 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 600 GB NVMe SSD for balanced general-purpose and database workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring AMD EPYC processors, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and a bundled 600 GB local NVMe SSD.

64.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
Bundled 600 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume
Average performance in multi-core benchmarks and database operations

Suited for moderate database tasks, web serving, and multi-threaded workloads
